channel.title: k8s on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in k8s on Frank Huang

item.title: k8s first try
item.description: Template for defining yaml file for orchestration: apiVersion:v1kind:Pod/Deploymentmetadata:name:labels:app:webSpec:replicas:2containers:- name:image:command:args:workingDir:ports:env:resources:volumeMounts:livenessProbe:readinessProbe:lifecycle:terminationMessagePath:imagePullPolicy:securityContext:stdin:stdinOnce:tty: use kubectl explain to check out term storage template for defining storage.yaml: kind: StorageClass apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1 metadata: name: jupyterhub-share namespace: jhub provisioner: kubernetes.io/no-provisioner what is the relationship between pv, pvc and storage? As (Gokhan Simsek)[https://softwareengineeringdaily.com/2019/01/11/why-is-storage-on-kubernetes-is-so-hard/] mentioned, there are two ways of dealing with storage: statically or dynamically. pv+pvc is for static provisioning. pvc+storage is for dynamic provisioning. In the previous pattern, one will have to define capacity in the pv.
